description:
As a user
  I want to interact with the knowledge graph visually
  So that I can explore and manage my notes intuitively

steps.name: the application is open on the graph view
there are no notes in the system
the graph canvas shows a message "No notes yet"
a "+" button is visible

elements.name: Create a new note via the plus button

steps.name: the application is open on the graph view
the graph view is active
I click the "+" button
a modal with a note form appears
I fill in "Title" with "My First Note"
I select type "Star"
I click "Save"
the modal closes
a new node labeled "My First Note" appears on the graph

elements.name: Edit a note from the graph

steps.name: the application is open on the graph view
a note "My First Note" exists on the graph
I click on the node "My First Note"
a side panel opens showing note details
I click "Edit" in the side panel
a modal with the note form appears pre-filled
I change "Title" to "Updated Note"
I click "Save"
the node label changes to "Updated Note"
the application is open on the graph view
a note "To Be Deleted" exists
I click on the node "To Be Deleted"
I click "Delete" in the side panel
a confirmation modal appears with text "Delete note?"
I confirm the deletion
the node "To Be Deleted" disappears from the graph

elements.name: Create a link by dragging between nodes

steps.name: the application is open on the graph view
notes "Source" and "Target" exist on the graph
I drag from node "Source" to node "Target"
a link preview line is shown
I release the mouse over "Target"
a weight selector appears
I select weight "4" and confirm
a new edge appears between "Source" and "Target"

elements.name: Search and focus on a node

steps.name: the application is open on the graph view
multiple notes exist on the graph
I click the search icon
I type "cosmos" in the search input
a list of matching notes appears
I click on a search result "Cosmos Article"
the graph zooms to the node "Cosmos Article"
the node is highlighted

name: Graph Navigation

description:
As a user
  I want to switch between 2D and 3D graph views
  So that I can choose between performance and visual appeal

steps.name: the application is open on the graph view
multiple notes exist on the graph
I am on the main page
the graph canvas shows "2D Mode" indicator
nodes are rendered as 2D shapes
the application is open on the graph view
multiple notes exist on the graph
I am on the graph view in 2D mode
I click the "3D View" button in floating controls
the graph switches to 3D mode
the graph canvas shows "3D Mode" indicator
nodes are rendered as 3D spheres
the application is open on the graph view
multiple notes exist on the graph
I am on the graph view in 3D mode
I click the "2D View" button
the graph switches back to 2D mode
the graph canvas shows "2D Mode" indicator
the application is open on the graph view
multiple notes exist on the graph
I am using a low-end device
I open the graph view
the graph defaults to 2D mode
the "3D View" button shows a warning icon
clicking it shows a performance warning
the application is open on the graph view
multiple notes exist on the graph
I am on the graph view in 2D mode
I scroll the mouse wheel up
the graph zooms in
I scroll the mouse wheel down
the graph zooms out
I drag the canvas
the graph pans in the dragged direction
the application is open on the graph view
multiple notes exist on the graph
I am on the graph view in 3D mode
I drag the mouse on the canvas
the camera rotates around the graph
I use the scroll wheel
the camera zooms in and out

elements.name: Click node to open details in 2D

steps.name: the application is open on the graph view
multiple notes exist on the graph
I am on the graph view in 2D mode
I click on a node
a side panel opens showing note details
the node is highlighted with a glow effect

elements.name: Click node to open details in 3D

steps.name: the application is open on the graph view
multiple notes exist on the graph
I am on the graph view in 3D mode
I click on a node
a side panel opens showing note details
the camera centers on the clicked node

name: Graph View Modes

description:
As a user
  I want to import external documents and export my knowledge graph

steps.name: the application is open on the graph view
I am on the graph view
I open the menu and select "Import"
I choose a Markdown file "notes.md"
I start the import
a progress indicator is shown
after completion, new nodes appear on the graph
the application is open on the graph view
I am on the graph view
I open the menu and select "Import"
I choose a PDF file "document.pdf"
I start the import
a progress indicator is shown
after completion, new nodes appear on the graph
the application is open on the graph view
I am on the graph view
I open the menu and select "Import"
I enter URL "https://example.com/article"
I start the import
a progress indicator is shown
after completion, new nodes appear on the graph
the application is open on the graph view
I have several notes on the graph
I open the menu and select "Export"
I choose format "JSON"
a file "knowledge_graph_export.json" is downloaded
the application is open on the graph view
I have several notes on the graph
I open the menu and select "Export"
I choose format "Markdown"
a file "knowledge_graph_export.md" is downloaded
the application is open on the graph view
I have several notes on the graph
I open the menu and select "Export"
I choose format "GraphML"
a file "knowledge_graph_export.graphml" is downloaded

name: Import and Export

description:
As a user
  I want to create, edit, and organize my notes
  So that I can build my knowledge graph

steps.name: the application is open
I am logged in as a registered user
I am creating a new note
I fill in "Title" with "Cosmos Overview"
I fill in "Content" with "The cosmos is vast. See also [[Galaxy Formation]]."
I select type "Galaxy"
I click "Save"
the note is saved
a node "Cosmos Overview" appears on the graph
a link to "Galaxy Formation" is created if it exists

elements.name: Create note with new wiki link creates placeholder

steps.name: the application is open
I am logged in as a registered user
I am creating a new note
I fill in "Content" with "Related to [[New Topic]]"
I click "Save"
the note is saved
a placeholder node "New Topic" appears on the graph
the placeholder is visually distinct (dashed border)
the application is open
I am logged in as a registered user
a note "Existing Note" exists
I open the note for editing
I change the content to "Updated content with [[Another Link]]"
I click "Save"
the note content is updated
new links are created based on wiki syntax
the application is open
I am logged in as a registered user
a note "My Note" of type "Planet" exists
I edit the note
I change the type to "Star"
I click "Save"
the node appearance changes to reflect the new type
the node size increases (stars are larger than planets)
the application is open
I am logged in as a registered user
notes of types "Star", "Planet", and "Comet" exist
I click the "Star" filter button
only "Star" type nodes are visible on the graph
I click the "All" filter button
all nodes are visible again
the application is open
I am logged in as a registered user
notes "Zebra", "Apple", and "Mango" exist
I select sort option "Alphabetical (A-Z)"
the notes are ordered "Apple", "Mango", "Zebra"
the application is open
I am logged in as a registered user
notes created on different dates exist
I select sort option "Newest first"
the most recently created note appears first
the application is open
I am logged in as a registered user
multiple notes exist on the graph
I hold Shift and click on 3 nodes
the 3 nodes are selected (highlighted)
I press Delete key
a confirmation modal appears
I confirm
all 3 selected nodes are deleted

name: Note Management

description:
As a user
  I want to search and discover related content
  So that I can find relevant notes quickly

steps.name: the application is open on the graph view
various notes exist in the system
I am on the graph view
I click the search icon in floating controls
a search input appears
I type "quantum"
a dropdown with matching notes appears
each result shows the note title and snippet
the application is open on the graph view
various notes exist in the system
I have searched for "physics"
search results are displayed
I click on a result "Quantum Physics"
the graph centers on the node "Quantum Physics"
the node pulses to indicate location
the side panel opens with note details
the application is open on the graph view
various notes exist in the system
notes with various content exist
I search for "relativity"
notes containing "relativity" in title or content are found
the matching text is highlighted in results
the application is open on the graph view
various notes exist in the system
semantic search is enabled
I search for "space exploration"
notes about "rockets", "mars missions", and "astronauts" are also found
results are ranked by relevance score
the application is open on the graph view
various notes exist in the system
I search for a term with no matches
I type "xyznonexistent"
a "No results found" message is displayed
suggestions for similar terms are shown
the application is open on the graph view
various notes exist in the system
I have performed searches before
I click on the search input
my recent searches are displayed
I click on a recent search
the search is executed again
the application is open on the graph view
various notes exist in the system
I have searched for "star"
I click the "Star" type filter
only "Star" type notes matching "star" are shown
I clear the filter
all notes matching "star" are shown
the application is open on the graph view
various notes exist in the system
I am viewing a note "Solar System"
I look at the side panel
a "Related Notes" section shows semantically similar notes
related notes are sorted by similarity score
the application is open on the graph view
various notes exist in the system
notes "A" and "Z" exist with multiple paths between them
I select node "A" and then Shift+click node "Z"
the shortest path between "A" and "Z" is highlighted
intermediate nodes on the path are emphasized

name: Search and Discovery
